heading: Oaths and acknowledgments
label: 4004 Oaths and acknowledgments
text: The wardens and superintendents, associate wardens and superintendents, chief clerks, and record clerks, of Federal penal or correctional institutions, may administer oaths to and take acknowledgments of officers, employees, and inmates of such institutions, but shall not demand or accept any fee or compensation therefor.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 754 (, §§ 1, 2, ).Feb. 11, 1938, ch. 2452 Stat. 28Section was extended to include superintendents and associate superintendents.Minor changes were made in phraseology. Words “the authority conferred by” were omitted as surplusage.

Amendments1984— substituted “and record clerks” for “record clerks, and parole officers”.Pub. L. 98–4731955—Act , permitted chief clerks, record clerks, and parole officers to administer oaths and take acknowledgments.July 7, 1955

Effective Date of 1984 AmendmentAmendment by effective , and applicable only to offenses committed after the taking effect of such amendment, see , set out as an Effective Date note under .Pub. L. 98–473section 235(a)(1) of Pub. L. 98–473section 3551 of this titleNov. 1, 1987
